name: Note Lane
description: A visual and logical lane that maps charted notes to specific input channels, spawn positions, scroll behavior, and hit targets.
design_notes: Note lanes turn chart data into readable action. The lane system should define note ids, lane ids, spawn timestamps, scroll behavior, hit target position, cleanup rules, and accessibility options for color, spacing, speed perception, and clutter reduction.
design_purpose: Organize dense chart data into readable input channels.
Help players anticipate upcoming actions through spatial layout.
Support difficulty scaling through lane count, note density, and visual clarity.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are reading a musical path and striking the correct lane at the right moment.

parameters.name: chart_id
parameters.description: Chart source containing lane and note definitions.

parameters.name: note_id
parameters.description: Chart note used for rendering, input matching, and replay.

parameters.name: note_lane_id
parameters.description: Input and visual lane assigned to the note.

parameters.name: note_spawn_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Audio-clock timestamp when the note should enter view.

parameters.name: chart_scroll_rate_id
parameters.description: Lane motion profile used to position upcoming notes.

parameters.name: hit_target_id
parameters.description: Interaction target where lane input is evaluated.

parameters.name: lane_accessibility_profile_id
parameters.description: Visual and input adjustments applied to lane presentation.

edge_cases: Two notes in the same lane overlap visually but need separate input windows.
A scroll-rate change occurs while notes are already visible.
A hold note crosses a pause boundary or tempo-map transition.
Colorblind lane mode changes visual identifiers but not input mapping.
A chart update changes note_id order and invalidates saved replay data.
common_bugs: Rendered note position drifts from audio-clock judgment because animation uses frame delta.
Lane input mapping changes in settings but existing spawned notes keep old lane ids.
Miss cleanup removes notes visually before the judgment system records the miss.
Hidden notes still block input in the same lane after being despawned.
Accessibility lane spacing changes hit-target placement but not collision or input zones.
balancing_notes: More lanes increase reading difficulty even if note density stays the same.
Scroll profiles should preserve anticipation; very fast lanes need strong calibration and practice support.
Lane colors, shapes, and spacing can lower cognitive load without reducing scoring strictness.
accessibility_notes: Provide high-contrast, colorblind-safe, enlarged-target, and reduced-clutter lane profiles.
Allow players to remap lane inputs and preview lane layout before songs.
Avoid relying only on color to distinguish simultaneous lanes.

implementation_notes.unity: Render notes from chart timestamps relative to AudioSettings.dspTime rather than moving them by accumulated frame delta. Keep note_id and note_lane_id in the spawned view model so misses, hits, replay, and accessibility layout all refer to the same chart data.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Control or Node2D lane containers driven by audio_playhead_ms. Input actions should map to note_lane_id through a settings layer, and note cleanup should be requested by the judgment system rather than animation completion.
implementation_notes.unreal: UMG or world-space lanes can read a rhythm chart model with note ids, lane ids, and spawn timestamps. Keep hit-target geometry synchronized with accessibility layout changes and use deterministic note ordering for replay.
implementation_notes.web: Use Canvas, SVG, or DOM rendering as a projection of chart data. Compute note positions from audio playhead and chart_scroll_rate_id each frame, while scoring operates on note timestamps and lane ids.

relationships.reason: Note lanes need timing windows to judge whether lane input hits, misses, or drops a note.
Lane labels and judgment text must scale without covering notes or reducing playfield readability.
Cooldown-style UI is usually inappropriate for note lanes because rhythm timing is driven by chart playback, not action recharge.

example_games.title: Dance Dance Revolution
example_games.note: Uses lane-like directional note tracks for timed input.

example_games.title: Guitar Hero
example_games.note: Uses a note highway with lane-specific inputs.
